questions.prompt: When piloting Printix Go on a printer, you should test sign-in and release with a regular User-role account, not just with the System manager account doing the install.
questions.explanation: Roles affect what a user can do at the printer. A System manager will pass tests that fail for a User. Test with the actual roles that will use the device (User, and Guest if applicable).

questions.prompt: Why does Printix Go installation often fail at first attempt on a printer that hasn't had per-vendor preparation?
choices: The printer's firmware downloads the Printix Go package incorrectly
Each vendor has documented prep steps (e.g. set printer password, install card reader, apply licence) that have to happen before Printix Go installs cleanly
Printix Go requires a full factory reset of the printer
The printer needs to be physically connected to a Windows computer to register
questions.explanation: The 'How to prepare X printer for Printix Go' step in the docs is the most-skipped step in the rollout. Skipping it produces 'Printix Go installation status is Failed' on the Printer's Printix Go tab.
